description: Treat the footer as a high-signal utility layer with grouped navigation, trust links, ecosystem routes, and practical controls rather than a thin afterthought.
evidence_notes: Railway and Notion both use dense multi-column footers to extend product trust and navigation.
Airbnb uses locale, legal, and support links as an important product utility layer.
PostHog keeps trust, compliance, and company depth visible in lower-page navigation.
rules: Group links by user job, not alphabetically.
Include trust, legal, and operational links where they support confidence.
Use the footer to extend the product ecosystem story, not just to dump navigation.
implementation_notes: Build with titled columns or grouped stacks that collapse cleanly on mobile.
Keep link labels short and scannable.
Support locale or currency controls when the product operates across markets.
avoid_when: The footer becomes a duplicate of the header without adding utility.
Link groups are so dense that scan time outweighs benefit.
Critical support or trust content is hidden in ambiguous labels.
